repo_prior_art.cite: Closest repo analogue is pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded: sharp bounded-manipulation RD interval; it is an RD partial-ID endpoint problem but not a selected LLM-cascade cutoff bound.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD give fixed-target smooth/discrete RD honest endpoint logic, not a population cost-quality selected cascade cutoff; sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.06028 and https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.04086.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PartialID entry states same-frontier non-representability of a selected local cascade value or a critical-curvature sign-certification index.

published_prior_art.cite: Kotte2026UCCI selects an isotonic calibrated threshold by constrained cost minimization and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt characterizes cascade frontier geometry; neither identifies nor separates the local boundary value V_*; sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18796 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06350.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives finite-atom active-set geometry for curvature-indexed selected endpoint bounds; pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded only gives a different bounded-manipulation endpoint envelope.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest indexes honest intervals by smoothness budgets but does not give finite-atom selected-cutoff active-set geometry; source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.01200.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo PartialID or Stat entry combines same-sample selected cascade cutoff inference with generated calibrated-score drift.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping, BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection, CalonicoCattaneoFarrell2018OptimalBandwidthRBC, DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError, and Pernot2023IsotonicStratification cover adjacent bandwidth-selection, post-selection, RD inference, generated-running-variable, or isotonic-mass issues, not this combined selected-threshold generated-score CI; sources include https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.0267, https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.01043, https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.00236, https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.07388, and https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05180.

novelty_flags.label: proposal-level comparator bookkeeping
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter handoff says the required SC6 comparator promise table is missing while the proposal names Kotte2026UCCI, Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference, KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD, and ZhangBenMichaelImai2022SafePolicyRD as comparators.

soundness_flags.label: conj:active-set-geometry
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed first boundary where an interpolation constraint switches from slack to binding is terminologically incoherent because the interpolation constraints in H_*(P,M) are equality constraints and are always binding.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Sharpness is conditional on the imposed C^2 range-respecting curvature-budget extension class, not derived from unrestricted observed-data primitives.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The selected endpoint interval is a field-tier sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:selection-margin / conj:postselect-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Positive separation of the selected population threshold is assumed rather than derived.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The honest-CI handle is conditional on an isolated selected threshold and does not cover ties or near-ties.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The inference rung is limited to the locally dense score regime and does not claim honest post-selection inference for genuinely atomic isotonic-score supports.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is only a conditional dense-regime open handle with generated-score drift correction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Emit the missing SC6 comparator promise table and map each named comparator to the exact statement that tightens, separates, reduces to, or merely uses it as background.
Repair Conjecture 1 by naming the actual active inequalities that can switch status, such as curvature, range, or dual optimal-recovery constraints, rather than interpolation equalities.
Add a small worked finite-atom example computing L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M), and M_*(P) from two or three support points to make the critical-curvature object auditable.

Sharpen Proposition 1 into an explicit same-K_P,Q_P,tau_* two-law or two-representative non-identification statement, so the separation from cascade frontier papers is statement-level rather than mostly prose-level.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the kernel is field-new and above the novelty floor, but comparator bookkeeping and the active-set statement need patching before Stage 0.
